description_long: This object is a vintage-style portable radio receiver with a boxy, rectangular green body and a metallic front panel. The front features a large analog meter with a needle, a rotary knob, a toggle switch, and a circular speaker grille, all arranged for easy access. A pair of over-ear headphones with a brown headband and black ear cups is attached to the top, with the cable plugged into a jack on the side. The top of the device has two black carrying straps and a metallic handle, suggesting portability. The back is plain except for the straps and a vented panel, while the sides show the headphone connection and a coiled cable. The overall design is robust, with visible screws and a utilitarian finish, suitable for field or urban use.
description: A vintage green portable radio receiver with a metallic front panel, analog meter, and attached over-ear headphones. Features include control knobs, carrying straps, and a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the analog meter, control knob, toggle switch, speaker grille, and the 'Radio' label.
description_view_1: Top view highlighting the brown headband and black ear cups of the headphones, as well as the carrying straps.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain green back, carrying straps, and vented panel at the bottom.
description_view_3: Side view showing the headphone jack, cable connection, and a partial view of the vented panel.
